### Languages

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_African_Republic&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Languages")]

Main article: [Languages of the Central African Republic](/wiki/Languages_of_the_Central_African_Republic "Languages of the Central African Republic")

The Central African Republic's two official languages are [French](/wiki/French_language "French language") and [Sango](/wiki/Sango_language "Sango language") (also spelled *Sangho*),[[151]](#cite_note-153) a [creole](/wiki/Creole_language "Creole language") developed as an inter-ethnic [lingua franca](/wiki/Lingua_franca "Lingua franca") based on the local [Ngbandi language](/wiki/Ngbandi_language "Ngbandi language"). Like Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger, Tanzania, Rwanda, Kenya, Uganda, South Africa and North African countries among others, Central African Republic is one of the African countries to have granted official status to an [African language](/wiki/Languages_of_Africa "Languages of Africa").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Religion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_African_Republic&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Religion")]

Main article: [Religion in the Central African Republic](/wiki/Religion_in_the_Central_African_Republic "Religion in the Central African Republic")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/91/Une_foule_devant_la_Cath%C3%A9drale_de_Bangui%2C_le_7_mai_2018.jpg/250px-Une_foule_devant_la_Cath%C3%A9drale_de_Bangui%2C_le_7_mai_2018.jpg)](/wiki/File:Une_foule_devant_la_Cath%C3%A9drale_de_Bangui,_le_7_mai_2018.jpg)

Worshippers at the [Bangui Cathedral](/wiki/Cath%C3%A9drale_Notre-Dame_(Bangui) "Cathédrale Notre-Dame (Bangui)"). [Christianity](/wiki/Christianity "Christianity") is the main religion in the Central African Republic.

According to the 2003 national census, 80.3% of the population was [Christian](/wiki/Christianity "Christianity") (51.4% [Protestant](/wiki/Protestantism "Protestantism") and 28.9% [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Roman_Catholicism "Roman Catholicism")), 10% was [Muslim](/wiki/Islam "Islam"), and 4.5 percent was other religious groups, with 5.5 percent having no religious beliefs.[[152]](#cite_note-154) More recent work from the Pew Research Center estimated that, as of 2010, Christians constituted 89.8% of the population (60.7% Protestant and 28.5% Catholic) while Muslims made up 8.9%.[[153]](#cite_note-Pew_Christians-155)[[154]](#cite_note-Pew_Muslims-156) The Catholic Church claims over 1.5 million adherents, approximately one-third of the population.[[155]](#cite_note-Catholic_Stats-157) Indigenous belief ([animism](/wiki/Animism "Animism")) is also practiced, and many indigenous beliefs are [incorporated into](/wiki/Religious_syncretism "Religious syncretism") Christian and Islamic practice.[[156]](#cite_note-158) A UN director described religious tensions between Muslims and Christians as being high.[[157]](#cite_note-159)

There are many missionary groups operating in the country, including [Lutherans](/wiki/Lutheran "Lutheran"), [Baptists](/wiki/Baptists "Baptists"), [Catholics](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic"), [Grace Brethren](/wiki/Fellowship_of_Grace_Brethren_Churches "Fellowship of Grace Brethren Churches"), and [Jehovah's Witnesses](/wiki/Jehovah%27s_Witnesses "Jehovah's Witnesses"). While these missionaries are predominantly from the United States, France, Italy, and Spain, many are also from [Nigeria](/wiki/Nigeria "Nigeria"), the [Democratic Republic of the Congo](/wiki/Democratic_Republic_of_the_Congo "Democratic Republic of the Congo"), and other African countries. Large numbers of missionaries left the country when fighting broke out between rebel and government forces in 2002–3, but many of them have now returned to continue their work.[[158]](#cite_note-160)

According to Overseas Development Institute research, during the crisis ongoing since 2012, religious leaders have mediated between communities and armed groups; they also provided refuge for people seeking shelter.[[122]](#cite_note-odi.org-124)

### Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_African_Republic&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Education")]

Main article: [Education in the Central African Republic](/wiki/Education_in_the_Central_African_Republic "Education in the Central African Republic")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/00/CAR_classroom.jpg/250px-CAR_classroom.jpg)](/wiki/File:CAR_classroom.jpg)

Classroom in [Sam Ouandja](/wiki/Sam_Ouandja "Sam Ouandja")

Public education in the Central African Republic is free and is compulsory from ages 6 to 14.[[159]](#cite_note-ilab-161) However, approximately half of the adult population of the country is [illiterate](/wiki/Literacy "Literacy").[[160]](#cite_note-162) The two institutions of higher education in the Central African Republic are the [University of Bangui](/wiki/University_of_Bangui "University of Bangui"), a public university located in Bangui, which includes a [medical school](/wiki/Medical_school "Medical school"); and [Euclid University](/wiki/Euclid_University "Euclid University"), an international university.[[161]](#cite_note-163)[[162]](#cite_note-164)

### Health

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_African_Republic&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Health")]

Main article: [Health in the Central African Republic](/wiki/Health_in_the_Central_African_Republic "Health in the Central African Republic")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9f/Caf_babies.jpg/250px-Caf_babies.jpg)](/wiki/File:Caf_babies.jpg)

Mothers with babies aged between 0 and 5 years are lining up in a Health Post at Begoua, a district of [Bangui](/wiki/Bangui "Bangui"), waiting for the two drops of the oral [polio vaccine](/wiki/Polio_vaccine "Polio vaccine").

The largest hospitals in the country are located in the Bangui district. As a member of the [World Health Organization](/wiki/World_Health_Organization "World Health Organization"), the Central African Republic receives vaccination assistance, such as a 2014 intervention for the prevention of a measles epidemic.[[163]](#cite_note-165) In 2007, female [life expectancy at birth](/wiki/Life_expectancy_at_birth "Life expectancy at birth") was 48.2 years, and male life expectancy at birth was 45.1 years.[[164]](#cite_note-hdrstats.undp.org-166)

[Women's health](/wiki/Women%27s_health "Women's health") is poor in the Central African Republic. As of 2010[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Central_African_Republic&action=edit), the country had the fourth highest [maternal mortality](/wiki/Maternal_mortality "Maternal mortality") rate in the world.[[165]](#cite_note-167) The [total fertility rate](/wiki/Total_fertility_rate "Total fertility rate") in 2014 was estimated at 4.46 children born/woman.[[15]](#cite_note-CIA-17) Approximately 25% of women had undergone [female genital mutilation](/wiki/Female_genital_mutilation "Female genital mutilation").[[166]](#cite_note-168) Many births in the country are guided by traditional [birth attendants](/wiki/Birth_attendant "Birth attendant"), who often have little or no formal training.[[167]](#cite_note-169)

[Malaria](/wiki/Malaria "Malaria") is endemic in the Central African Republic and one of the leading causes of death.[[168]](#cite_note-170) According to 2009 estimates, the HIV/AIDS [prevalence](/wiki/Prevalence "Prevalence") rate is about 4.7% of the adult population (ages 15–49).[[169]](#cite_note-171) This is in general agreement with the 2016 [United Nations](/wiki/United_Nations "United Nations") estimate of approximately 4%.[[147]](#cite_note-unaids-149) Government expenditure on health was US$20 (PPP) per person in 2006[[164]](#cite_note-hdrstats.undp.org-166) and 10.9% of total government expenditure in 2006.[[164]](#cite_note-hdrstats.undp.org-166) There was only around 1 physician for every 20,000 people in 2009.[[170]](#cite_note-172)

With a score of 31.5 in the 2024 Global Hunger Index,[[171]](#cite_note-173) the Central African Republic ranks 119th out of the 127 countries with sufficient data to calculate 2024 GHI scores.

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_African_Republic&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Culture")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b0/BaAka_woman_dancing.jpg/250px-BaAka_woman_dancing.jpg)](/wiki/File:BaAka_woman_dancing.jpg)

A Central African woman

See also: [List of African writers by country § Central African Republic](/wiki/List_of_African_writers_by_country#Central_African_Republic "List of African writers by country"), and [Music of the Central African Republic](/wiki/Music_of_the_Central_African_Republic "Music of the Central African Republic")

The nation comprises over 80 ethnic groups, each having its own language. The largest ethnic groups are the Baggara Arabs, Baka, Banda, Bayaka, Fula, Gbaya, Kara, Kresh, Mbaka, Mandja, Ngbandi, Sara, Vidiri, Wodaabe, Yakoma, Yulu, and Zande, with others including Europeans of mostly French descent.

### Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_African_Republic&action=edit&section=30 "Edit section: Sports")]

See also: [Central African Republic at the Olympics](/wiki/Central_African_Republic_at_the_Olympics "Central African Republic at the Olympics")

Football is the country's most popular sport. The [national football team](/wiki/Central_African_Republic_national_football_team "Central African Republic national football team") is governed by the [Central African Football Federation](/wiki/Central_African_Football_Federation "Central African Football Federation") and stages matches at the [Barthélemy Boganda Stadium](/wiki/Barth%C3%A9lemy_Boganda_Stadium "Barthélemy Boganda Stadium").[[172]](#cite_note-174)

Basketball also is popular[[173]](#cite_note-175)[[174]](#cite_note-176) and its [national team](/wiki/Central_African_Republic_national_basketball_team "Central African Republic national basketball team") won the [African Championship](/wiki/AfroBasket "AfroBasket") twice and was the first Sub-Saharan African team to qualify for the [Basketball World Cup](/wiki/Basketball_World_Cup "Basketball World Cup"), in [1974](/wiki/1974_FIBA_World_Championship "1974 FIBA World Championship").
